title: Interactive Dose-Response Curve Explorer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive p5.js MicroSim for exploring dose-response curves, LD50 values, threshold effects, and toxicological principles with multiple substances.

learningObjectives: Interpret dose-response curves to identify LD50 values and threshold effects
Compare relative toxicity of different substances using LD50 values
Explain the concept 'the dose makes the poison'
Distinguish between threshold and non-threshold dose-response models
prerequisites: Basic understanding of graphs with logarithmic scales
Introduction to the concept of toxicity
Familiarity with concentration and dosage units

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Interactive parameter manipulation with multiple substances
keyQuestions: Which substance has the lowest LD50 and what does that mean for its toxicity?
How does curve steepness affect the range of doses that are 'somewhat dangerous'?
Why do threshold effects matter for setting environmental safety standards?
Is a dose just below the LD50 truly safe?
commonMisconceptions: Natural substances are always safer than synthetic ones
Any amount of a toxic substance is dangerous (ignoring dose dependence)
LD50 means that any dose below it is completely safe
assessmentOpportunities: Toxicity ranking exercise using LD50 comparisons
Risk assessment writing for a real-world pollution scenario
Diagram labeling of dose-response curve features
